title: rubus nemoralis subsp. microphyllus
intro: Rubus nemoralis subsp. microphyllus, also known as the dwarf woodland bramble or small-leaved woodland bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus nemoralis plant, a type of wild raspberry native to Europe. The leaves and stems of this plant have been used in traditional medicine due to their rich content of antioxidants and tannins, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Rubus nemoralis subsp. microphyllus have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
